creator: joel.faedi@gmail.com
text:
sysinstall allows you to choose during and after initial setup a NTP server ntpdate uses at boot time. This is done by setting parameters in /etc/rc.conf for /etc/rc.d/ntpdate which is launched at boot time. 

In older version of FreeBSD, ntpdate_flags was used. In newer versions of FreeBSD (almost 7.3, 8.1-RCx and 9.0-CURRENt), the new startup script "/etc/rc.d/ntpdate" expects the NTP server name in ntpdate_hosts as described in /etc/defaults/rc.conf (otherwise it uses default values from /etc/ntp.conf).

Fix: Apply the following patch in /usr/src/usr.sbin/sysinstall 
It modifies sysinstall.h, config.c, menus.c and sysinstall.8


Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: 1. launch sysinstall and select a NTP server for ntpdate
2. have a look in /etc/rc.conf
3. the chosen server is set in ntpdate_flags instead of ntpdate_hosts
